issuesFound.title: button missing accessible name and expanded state

issuesFound.title: toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop

issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them. Users who cannot perceive colour differences (e.g., colour blindness) cannot identify links.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition on hover but lacks a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from this unexpected movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 760: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1792: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
        Line 1800: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the expanded state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1955: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
        Line 1968: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates visible product counts and grid content but does not announce these changes via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know that filtering results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2507: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Filter applied: ${_productFilterState.activeCategory}`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid input elements. Screen reader users will not hear that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4042: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1889: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-controls on the navigation toggle button pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1889: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not set aria-hidden on the drawer element. This leaves the hidden drawer in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1792: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);
        Line 1800: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._onTabClick function handles filter tab clicks and updates the product grid but does not announce the filter change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2535: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(value) : `Filter applied: ${value}`);

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick is attached to a non-button element (if _toggleEl is not a button) but only handles click events. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle with Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3398: Add keydown event handler for Enter and Space keys that calls _onToggleClick.

issuesFound.title: Modal/dialog missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function opens a navigation drawer but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer or activate a focus trap. Keyboard users remain trapped in the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1792: const trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); trap.activate();

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Toggle function mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function changes the visible state of the drawer but does not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: In the open function, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2850: In the close function, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot track the expand/collapse state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In the open function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 4010: In the close function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update productFilter.applyFilters not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter updates the visible card count and grid but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know the results have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In applyFilters, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches));`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation function inlineValidator._renderResult does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be alerted to the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: In _renderResult, when `result.valid` is false, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`. When valid, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for mobileNav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on DOMContentLoaded. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: In the init function, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for dropdownMenu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: In the register function, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element mobileNav drawer not updated with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS classes but aria-hidden is not set. Screen reader users may still encounter the hidden content, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: In the open function, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`. In the close function, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element dropdownMenu panel not updated with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden via the hidden attribute but aria-hidden is not synchronized. Screen reader users may encounter inconsistent state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`. In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`.

issuesFound.title: User action pricing.setPeriod not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know the prices have changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In setPeriod, after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: User action productFilter._onTabClick not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a product filter tab updates the grid but does not announce the applied filter via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know the filter has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In _onTabClick, after `_productFilterState.activeCategory = value;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(value));`.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element pricing._toggleEl missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element (if not a button) has a click listener but no keydown handler for Enter/Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In init, add a keydown event listener to _toggleEl that checks for `e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE` and calls `_onToggleClick`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error state not cleared in inlineValidator._renderResult
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to false. Screen reader users may continue to hear an invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: In _renderResult, when `result.valid` is true, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function testimonialsCarousel.goTo does not update aria-selected on dots
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel navigation dots have role="tab" but aria-selected is not updated when the slide changes. Screen reader users cannot track the current slide, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300: In goTo, in the `_syncDots` function, ensure each dot's aria-selected is set based on `i === target`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update siteSearch._fetchAndRender not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Search suggestions are updated dynamically but no live region announces the result count. Screen reader users may not know suggestions are available, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: In _fetchAndRender, after `_updateStatus(results.length);`, add a live region announcement if results.length > 0.

issuesFound.title: User action faqAccordion.toggle not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling an FAQ item expands/collapses the answer but does not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know the content changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In toggle, after changing the open state, add `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls missing on mobileNav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify the controlled region, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: In init, after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update newsletterForm._onSubmit not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Newsletter form submission shows success/error messages but does not announce them via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may miss the feedback, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: In _onSubmit, after the try/catch block, ensure the status element has aria-live="polite" and is focused or announced via `liveRegion.announce`.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1148: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the `_overlay` element using the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update `aria-hidden`. This can lead to inconsistent accessibility tree states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: `_overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`, Line 1153: `_overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle` action (via `open` and `close`) changes the visible state of the primary navigation, but there is no corresponding write to an `aria-live` region to announce "Navigation menu opened" or "closed".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1148: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function cycles the carousel between playing and paused states, but it does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the `_playBtn`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1788: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function changes the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` inside the `toggle()` function (or inside `open()` and `close()` so the attribute reflects the current state).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element (`.nav-toggle`) is used to open/close the navigation drawer but never receives an `aria‑controls` attribute that points to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it manipulates.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"` ) and add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer');` during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger Escape key does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the Escape key is pressed on a dropdown trigger, the panel is closed via `_closeInstance()`, but the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is not set to `false`. This leaves the accessibility state stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the Escape‑key handling branch, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands an FAQ item and animates the panel, but it never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated button. The button’s `aria‑expanded` remains `false`, misleading screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._fetchAndRender()` populates the suggestion list but does not write the result (e.g., “5 results found”) to any element with `role="status"` or `aria‑live`. Users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback that suggestions have changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After rendering, set `_statusEl.textContent = `${count} result${count===1?'':'s'} found`;` and ensure `_statusEl` has `role="status"` or `aria‑live="polite"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the email field with `validateField()` but never marks the input with `aria‑invalid="true"` when the value is invalid. Screen‑reader users cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `newsletterForm._onSubmit()` when `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on successful validation.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element. Assistive technologies may still expose the hidden navigation to users.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `mobileNav.open()` remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` hides the panel by adding a CSS class and setting `hidden`, but it never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` on the panel. The panel remains perceivable to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove the attribute when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the site theme (`[data-theme-toggle]`) changes the theme but never updates `aria‑pressed` to reflect its on/off state. Users cannot know which theme is active.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the theme, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isDark));` where `isDark` represents the current theme.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the displayed prices when the billing period changes, but it does not provide an audible announcement (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). Users of screen readers receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, set `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + period);`.

---

fpTitles: Dropdown panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu add/remove CSS classes and hide/show panels but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Site‑search autocomplete does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _siteSearch module shows and hides the suggestion list via suggestionListRenderer.show()/hide() but never toggles aria‑expanded on the search <input>, so assistive technologies cannot tell whether suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix: When opening the list call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and when closing call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion open/close functions animate panels but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle ( [data‑billing‑toggle] ) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed or aria‑checked, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive its on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick and _ onToggleKeydown call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)) or use aria‑checked for a switch role.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product grid, count badge, and empty‑state but does not announce the result to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: After updating, call liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`) or similar.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion open/close emit custom events but do not use a live region to announce panel state changes.
issuesFound.fix: After open(item) call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – expanded`) and after close(item) announce collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid via error messages but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so AT cannot detect errors.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when invalid and remove when valid.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error (contact form name)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7 for the contactForm name input; missing aria‑invalid prevents error detection.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 7.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the dropdown trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute is not set (defaults to undefined), violating ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In register after creating trigger, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> does not have aria‑expanded set to false initially, breaking disclosure semantics.
issuesFound.fix: After mounting the listbox, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons lack an initial aria‑expanded state, so screen readers cannot know they are collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when item.open is false.

issuesFound.title: Visibility‑toggled product cards do not update aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: filterAnimation adds hidden attribute but never toggles aria‑hidden, leaving hidden cards still perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In filterAnimation.run when hiding a card, also setAttr(card, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and when showing remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed or aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle (switch) changes visual state but lacks aria‑pressed or aria‑checked, violating ARIA for switches.
issuesFound.fix: In _ onToggleClick set setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)) or aria‑pressed.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons do not reflect state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: _prevBtn and _nextBtn are enabled/disabled but lack aria‑pressed or aria‑disabled updates, so AT cannot detect availability.
issuesFound.fix: When disabling/enabling, call setAttr(button, 'aria-disabled', String(isDisabled)).

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: fileUploadField adds hidden attribute to the list but does not set aria‑hidden, leaving hidden file list exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding the list, also setAttr(listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and remove when showing.

issuesFound.fix: Line 959: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot tell when the table region is focused for scrolling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the active question.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. The accent-color does not provide a focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Focus is invisible in the footer area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1207: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard navigation is obscured.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1216: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Focus state is not visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1224: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which renders content visibly in the layout instead of hiding it. This clutters the UI with text meant for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses words together. This reduces legibility, especially for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 223: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Links in body text (e.g., .faq-item dd p a) rely solely on colour to be identified, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: .section-header p uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-page (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is 2.85:1, below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 623: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #cbd5e1 / background #555555 ratio 1.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] uses --colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555). The contrast ratio is 1.3:1, making text nearly illegible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1203: footer[role="contentinfo"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 2.1:1
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555). The contrast ratio is 2.1:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.6:1
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-bottom > p uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555). The contrast ratio is 1.6:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: .footer-bottom > p { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px. This is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: .btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the mandatory 24px minimum and recommended 44px size, creating a significant barrier for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 463: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets 20px dimensions. This is below the 24px minimum, making icon buttons extremely difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px × 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size, risking missed taps for users on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 572: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized at 20px × 20px. This is below the 24px minimum, making them difficult to select for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1191: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: .skip-link uses transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 413 is empty and does not disable this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img uses transition: transform for a 3D effect. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 722 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same block. This removes the focus ring for all keyboard users, making navigation impossible for those who rely on keyboard input.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule removes the outline without a visible alternative. Users navigating the mobile menu via keyboard cannot see which element is active.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search input without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule overrides the :focus rule that had a box-shadow, setting outline: none with no visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus in the search field.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within the footer navigation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This clutters the visual interface.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 533 is empty and does not disable the motion. This can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from links. Relying solely on color to distinguish links from text fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses #aaaaaa text on a #ffffff background, resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.9:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement for normal text, affecting users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p uses #64748b text on a #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of 1.3:1. This is extremely low and makes the text illegible for many users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links use #94a3b8 text on a #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.6:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on base button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum size for touch targets. This is a critical barrier for motor-impaired users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum size. Icon buttons are particularly hard to hit at this size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] sets height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum size. This makes form controls difficult to select on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle sets min-height and min-width to 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Mobile navigation toggles should be large for easy access.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Input fields should be tall enough for easy touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links set min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for optimal touch accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on header logo link without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the site logo link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on submenu links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus cannot see focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tabs without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter tab.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the selected billing cycle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify which FAQ item is focused.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle the visible state of the drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1390: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1420: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation init function sets up event listeners but does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to false. Assistive technologies may not announce the initial closed state correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown and hidden via CSS classes and hidden attribute, but the aria-hidden attribute is not toggled accordingly. Screen readers may still navigate to the hidden menu content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1390: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1420: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger click handler toggles the menu state but does not announce the change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1363: liveRegion.announce(_open ? STRINGS.announce.navOpen : STRINGS.announce.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1620: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1640: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu register function sets aria-haspopup but does not initialize aria-expanded to false on the trigger. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1700: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel visibility is toggled using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not explicitly managed. This may cause inconsistencies in how different assistive technologies perceive the panel's availability.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1620: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1640: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded to true on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions toggle the panel visibility but do not explicitly manage the aria-hidden attribute on the panel element.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion container click handler toggles the item state but does not announce the change (e.g., "Answer expanded") to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the content change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2683: liveRegion.announce(item.open ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed : STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain announced as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates prices and UI states but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the price update.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop button visibility is toggled via CSS classes and hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated. Screen readers may attempt to navigate to the button when it is visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition lacks motion reduction override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition lacks motion reduction override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img class includes transform in its transition property for a 3D rotation effect. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1523 is empty and does not disable this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1523: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } }

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform transition lacks motion reduction override

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally without ensuring that all link states provide a non-colour visual differentiator. Many link instances in the stylesheet (navigation, footer, social links) rely solely on colour changes, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 172: a { text-decoration: underline; } or alternatively add text-decoration: underline to :hover and :focus states for all link selectors that currently lack them.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without providing a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets only outline-color while outline remains none, resulting in no visible focus indicator because outline-style is not defined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 276: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation links and buttons

issuesFound.fix: Line 356: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on submenu links

issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn sets min-height: 32px, meeting the 24px minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended target size for buttons.

issuesFound.fix: Line 543: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size for comfortable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 647: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-toggle button sets min-height: 28px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 786: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px, exactly at the minimum but below the 44px recommended size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size for social media links.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen reader text will be visible on screen, disrupting the visual layout while remaining accessible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement animations that are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 495: Add after line 495: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .skip-link includes transform in its transition property, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 259: Add after line 259: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .product-card includes transform in its transition property for hover effects, which is not disabled in the reduced motion media query, potentially causing accessibility issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 694: Add after line 694: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .stat-item includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on hover that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 752: Add after line 752: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: background-color var(--transition-base); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-card includes transform in its transition property, creating motion effects that are not disabled for users with motion sensitivity preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 813: Add after line 813: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making words run together visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: Remove the property or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-primary uses background-color #5588bb with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: `background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (or `#004aaa` which provides ~4.5:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-outline
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-outline uses color #5588bb on a transparent background (white), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1 against the page background, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (or `#004aaa`)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-ghost
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-ghost uses color #5588bb on transparent background (white), providing only ~3.1:1 contrast ratio, which fails the WCAG AA requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (or `#004aaa`)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-danger uses background-color #ee9999 with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 521: `background-color: var(--colour-error-dark);` (or `#991b1b` which provides sufficient contrast)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a removes text underlines and only changes color on hover, relying solely on color to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1101: Add `text-decoration: underline;` to the hover state, or Line 1100: Add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a removes underlines and provides only color change on hover, making links indistinguishable from text for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to the base rule at line 436

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a removes text underlines and relies solely on color change for hover states, failing to provide a non-color visual differentiator required by WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1111: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or Line 1110: Add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open to true, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", this mismatch means screen readers will report the panel as collapsed even when it is visually open.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the _renderResult function calls srUtils.markInvalid which adds aria-describedby linking to the error message, but neither function sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Consequently, screen readers do not announce the field as invalid, and users may not realize their input contains errors.
issuesFound.fix:
In _renderResult (else branch) after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`: Line: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
  In the valid branch (after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`): Line: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu not initialising aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menu triggers are registered during initialization, the code sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup on the trigger, and hides the panel using the hidden attribute, but it fails to initialise aria-expanded="false". Screen readers may assume the dropdown is expanded by default, causing confusion on first interaction.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch not initialising aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load. Screen readers may incorrectly report the combobox as expanded when the page first loads.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element (_drawer). The drawer remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden, allowing screen readers to navigate to off-screen content. Conversely, when opened, aria-hidden is not removed.
issuesFound.fix:
In close() after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`: Line: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`
  In open() after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`: Line: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters and updating the product grid, the code updates the count badge and emits a custom event but does not announce the results via an aria-live region. Screen reader users receive no feedback about how many products match the selected filters or whether the grid has updated.

issuesFound.title: pricing module not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually via animations, but no announcement is made via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu not announcing open/close state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no status announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. While focus management helps, users benefit from confirmation that the menu has expanded or collapsed, especially when using hover or click interactions that do not move focus into the menu.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`: Line: `liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');`
  In _closeInstance after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`: Line: `liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1458 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line ~1473 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the search suggestions listbox but do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl). Screen readers cannot report whether the suggestion list is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1990 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line ~1998 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: During mobileNav.init(), the trigger button (_trigger) is discovered and event listeners are attached, but the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false". Screen readers may report the state as undefined or inconsistent when the page loads with the menu closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1375 (in init): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdownMenu.register() initializes a dropdown pair, it ensures aria-haspopup is set but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger button, even though the panel is initially hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1550 (in register): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: In siteSearch.init(), the search input is configured with aria-controls and role="combobox", but the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" even though the suggestion list starts hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2028 (in init): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation not clearing aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference for error messages but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute from the field. Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~843 (in markValid): Add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes the open CSS class from the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element (_drawer). Screen readers may still be able to access the closed navigation content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1348 (in close): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` Line ~1328 (in open): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". While hidden removes the element from the accessibility tree in most browsers, explicit aria-hidden provides clearer intent and better support for custom display logic.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1473 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` Line ~1458 (in _openInstance): Add `removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.hide() function sets the hidden attribute on the listbox but does not set aria-hidden="true". Additionally, the show() function does not remove aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1968 (in hide): Add `setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` Line ~1962 (in show): Add `removeAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid, count badges, and empty state, but does not announce these changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the filter results have been updated or how many products are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2158 (in applyFilters callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied.replace('{count}', String(totalMatches)));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays across all plan cards when the billing period changes, but does not announce this change via a live region. Users of assistive technologies may not notice that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2497 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle uses role="switch" (line ~2527) but the _syncToggleState() and _onToggleClick() functions only toggle CSS classes and do not update the aria-checked attribute to reflect the on/off state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2517 (in _syncToggleState): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear);` where isYear is a boolean indicating if the period is annual.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the active CSS class on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-selected="true" on the active tab or aria-selected="false" on inactive tabs. Since these function as tabs, the selected state must be communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2182 (in _syncTabAriaStates): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion items missing aria-selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex() function updates the visual active state of suggestion items but does not update the aria-selected attribute on the option elements. The aria-selected attribute remains "false" (as set in the initial HTML template) even when an item is actively selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1960 (in setActiveIndex): Add `setAttr(item, ARIA.SELECTED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: During mobileNav.init(), the trigger button is not given an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's ID. This prevents screen reader users from understanding which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1375 (in init): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not announce the state change ("Navigation opened" or "Navigation closed") via the live region, nor do they move focus into the drawer when opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1328 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` Line ~1348 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is opened by adding CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to true. Screen readers cannot announce that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2183: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to false, leaving screen readers thinking the menu is still open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2208: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation trigger button controls the drawer element but lacks an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer's ID, preventing screen readers from identifying the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2255: Insert `if (_drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed because it only removes CSS classes without setting aria-hidden or the hidden attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2218: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)` and Line 2186: Insert `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` after `addClass(_drawer, ...)`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation is a significant context change that is not announced to screen readers via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2190: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` Line 2220: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it, leaving keyboard users stranded on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2192: Insert `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is shown but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to true.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2353: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2373: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger button is given aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not aria-expanded="false" for the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2358: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened);` Line 2378: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is shown but the combobox input's aria-expanded attribute is never set to true.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3203: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is hidden but the combobox input's aria-expanded attribute is never set to false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3233: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the field is not marked with aria-invalid="true", so screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3760: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, aria-invalid is not set to false or removed, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3755: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) indicate active state visually via CSS but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4256: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch element missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element has role="switch" but its aria-checked attribute is never updated to reflect the current period (year vs month).

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices visually but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4240: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after `_syncToggleState(period);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter _onTabClick missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu but does not expose its expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the sub-menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: NavDropdown component: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button expands and collapses sub-sections in the mobile navigation but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen-reader users from knowing the current state of the section.
issuesFound.fix: Line: MobileNav component: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>
