issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls the answer panel with aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1744: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element lacks the corresponding id. This creates a broken ARIA reference that prevents screen readers from announcing the error message when it appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form name field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-name-error', but FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-name-error", so the ErrorMessage lacks the required id attribute, breaking the programmatic association.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1930: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is missing the errorId prop, resulting in a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is missing the errorId prop, breaking the connection to the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form message field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is missing the errorId prop, preventing screen readers from associating the error message with the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} hintId="contact-message-hint" errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual toggle buttons indicate the selected billing period but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1694 (Monthly button): `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={[ 'billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`
  Fix: Line 1701 (Annual button): `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={[ 'billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation has a visual indicator (nav-link--current class) but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation has a visual current state but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button shows a spinner and disables interaction, but lacks aria-busy to inform screen readers that the element is loading and content may change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 890: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a navigation landmark but lacks an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify its purpose when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label, making it harder for screen reader users to identify the section when navigating by regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1278: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: Statistics section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar is a distinct page section with a heading, but the section element lacks aria-labelledby to expose the heading as its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1418: `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: Pricing card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element containing a heading, but lacks aria-labelledby to expose the plan name as the card's accessible name in screen reader card navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1656: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: Pricing card CTA link missing contextual accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The "Get started" link text is identical across all pricing cards. Without an aria-label including the plan name, screen reader users cannot distinguish between the different plan CTAs when navigating by links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: `<a href={plan.ctaHref} className={[ 'btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name} plan`}>`

issuesFound.title: Integration marquee clone not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integrations for visual marquee scrolling is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Navigation dropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) is decorative text content inside a button that already has a text label, but lacks aria-hidden, causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1053: `<span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the section toggle button is decorative but lacks aria-hidden, resulting in redundant screen reader announcements alongside the button label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: `{isExpanded ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the accordion trigger is decorative but lacks aria-hidden, causing it to be announced alongside the question text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1756: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="faq-item__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is decorative (accompanied by visually hidden text), but lacks aria-hidden, potentially causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", so screen reader users may not realize the content behind the dialog is inert while the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: `<div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form message hint not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The hint text "Up to 1200 characters" is visible but not associated with the textarea via aria-describedby because the hintId prop is missing from FormGroup.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} hintId="contact-message-hint" errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces bare asterisk to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk indicating required fields is rendered as plain text without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" which may be confusing without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 956: ` *`
